description.task: Create an abstract Shape class with an abstract method area(). Implement it in Triangle and Square subclasses.
instructions: Implement Triangle(String color, double base, double height) with area() returning 0.5 * base * height.
Implement Square(String color, double side) with area() returning side * side.
Do NOT change AbstractDemo.main() or the abstract Shape class.
Note: you cannot instantiate Shape directly — only its concrete subclasses.
description.hint: Abstract classes can have constructors but cannot be instantiated with 'new Shape(...)'. Subclass constructors pass values to the parent with super(color).
description.expectedOutput:
Color: Red, Area: 12.00
Color: Blue, Area: 25.00

description.task: Create a Printable interface with a print() method. Implement it in two classes: Document and Photo.
description.context: An interface is a contract that forces implementing classes to provide specific methods. Unlike abstract classes, interfaces support multiple implementation.
instructions: Implement Document with constructor Document(String title) and implement print().
Implement Photo with constructor Photo(String filename, int width, int height) and implement print().
description.hint: Classes use 'implements InterfaceName'. All interface methods are implicitly public — your implementations must also be public.
description.expectedOutput:
Printing document: Annual Report
Printing photo: vacation.jpg (1920x1080)

description.task: Create two interfaces (Flyable and Swimmable) and a Duck class that implements both. A Penguin class implements only Swimmable.
description.context: Java does not support inheriting from multiple classes, but a class can implement multiple interfaces. This is a key advantage of interfaces over abstract classes.
instructions: Implement Duck implementing both Flyable and Swimmable.
Observe that a Penguin can be referenced as Swimmable but cannot call fly().
description.hint: Multiple interface syntax: class Duck implements Flyable, Swimmable { ... }
description.expectedOutput:
Duck is flying.
Duck is swimming.
Penguin is swimming.
